description.company: Linus Health is a Boston-based digital health company focused on transforming brain health for people across the world. By advancing how we detect and address cognitive and brain disorders – leveraging cutting-edge neuroscience, clinical expertise, and artificial intelligence – our goal is to enable a future where people can live longer, happier, and healthier lives with better brain health.
description.role:
The Role:

We are seeking a Senior Product Designer to help shape and deliver high-quality digital product experiences at Linus Health. 

This role will support a range of patient, participant, clinician, and partner-facing experiences across our product ecosystem. You will work across mobile and responsive web experiences, partner closely with Product, Engineering, Research, Clinical, and Commercial teams, and help translate complex health information into clear, supportive user experiences.

This is a strong fit for a senior designer who can move between strategy and execution, work comfortably in ambiguity, and deliver high-quality product design in a fast-moving environment.

What you'll do

  • Lead design for key product experiences across Linus Health’s digital ecosystem, including design for a new consumer health experience focused on brain health education and engagement.
  • Translate early product strategy, concept wireframes, business goals, and technical requirements into clear, usable product experiences
  • Create high-quality mobile and responsive web designs that support multiple consumer and partner entry points
  • Partner with Product Management to define MVP scope, clarify problem statements, shape product requirements, and prioritize the highest-value user experiences
  • Collaborate closely with Engineering to understand technical constraints, platform dependencies, assessment requirements, wearable integrations, and delivery tradeoffs
  • Work within Linus’s existing design system while thoughtfully extending patterns for consumer engagement
  • Create prototypes for usability testing, stakeholder alignment, executive review, and engineering handoff
  • Partner with UX Research to plan, test, and iterate on concepts 
  • Apply accessibility, cognitive accessibility, and plain-language principles to designs
  • Balance speed, quality, and ambiguity while supporting a fast-moving, cross-functional build team
  • Document user flows, interaction patterns, edge cases, and design decisions in Figma, Jira, and Confluence
  • Participate in design critiques and collaborate within a small but growing design team

About You:

Must Haves:

  • Senior-level product design experience (5+ years), ideally in consumer health, digital health, healthcare, wellness, life sciences, or complex regulated products
  • Strong portfolio demonstrating end-to-end product design across mobile and responsive web experiences
  • Mastery of Figma, including designing within and extending established design systems
  • Strong interaction design, information architecture, and visual design skills
  • Comfort operating in ambiguous product spaces and using design to bring structure, clarity, and momentum
  • Familiarity with product development tools such as Jira and Confluence
  • Strong communication skills, including the ability to explain design rationale to technical, clinical, product, and executive stakeholders
  • High-level working knowledge of regulated product considerations and boundaries (e.g. the distinction between wellness and clinical experiences) with the judgment to flag when Legal or Compliance input is needed
  • Ability to move quickly while maintaining a high bar for usability and accessibility

Nice To Haves:

  • Experience with mobile app design, native app patterns, push notification strategies, or wearable-integrated experiences
  • Experience designing healthcare consent, privacy, data-sharing, or regulated workflows
  • Familiarity with behavioral nudging, longitudinal engagement, habit formation, or personalized content delivery
  • Experience designing white-labeled, partner-configurable, or multi-channel product experiences
  • Experience supporting usability testing or mixed-methods research
  • Thoughtful use of AI tools to improve design workflows, ideation, prototyping, synthesis, or documentation, with appropriate attention to privacy and compliance

Compensation for US based candidates: The base salary budgeted for this position is in the $150,000-170,000 range per year. This position will also include a yearly target discretionary bonus as well as equity, health benefits, unlimited PTO, a monthly flex day, a 401K plan with a match and more. The final offer determined for the candidate who is hired into this position will depend on a number of factors, including, but not limited to, the candidate's relevant skills, professional experience, labor market conditions, etc. 


Linus Health is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified candidates will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity or expression, age, genetic information, disability or any characteristic protected by law. We believe that diversity is critical to the growth of our company and understand the importance of fostering an environment where everyone has a voice. We are also committed to providing reasonable accommodations for candidates with disabilities during the recruiting process. If you are in need of assistance due to a disability, please contact us
